title: Fast analyze for GitHub issues
story: As a framework user, I want `/isdlc analyze #N` to show Maya's first message within seconds, so that the analyze flow feels conversational rather than broken.
acceptance_criteria: Maya's first message appears in approximately 11 seconds instead of 90 seconds
The conversation quality is identical to the current roundtable experience
All analysis artifacts are produced with the same content and structure as today

title: No confirmation prompt for external refs
story: As a framework user, I want `/isdlc analyze #42` to automatically add the issue to the backlog without asking me to confirm, so that I don't waste time on an obvious intent.
acceptance_criteria: No 'Add to backlog?' prompt appears for #N inputs
No 'Add to backlog?' prompt appears for PROJECT-N inputs
Non-external-ref inputs still prompt for confirmation when no match is found

title: No duplicate issue fetch
story: As a framework user, I want the system to fetch my GitHub/Jira issue only once, so that the pre-dispatch pipeline doesn't waste time on redundant network calls.
acceptance_criteria: gh issue view (or Jira equivalent) is called exactly once per analyze invocation
The add handler receives pre-fetched data instead of re-fetching
The add handler still fetches independently when invoked via /isdlc add

title: Alex contributes codebase evidence from exchange 2
story: As a framework user, I want Alex to contribute codebase-grounded observations starting from the second exchange, so that the conversation has technical depth without delaying Maya's opening.
acceptance_criteria: Maya's first message does not wait for codebase scan completion
Alex's first contribution includes evidence from the codebase scan
Alex contributes no later than exchange 2 (assuming scan completes during exchange 1 processing)

title: Backward-compatible roundtable dispatch
story: As a framework maintainer, I want the roundtable-analyst to fall back to reading files when the dispatch prompt does not include inlined context, so that existing invocations and tests continue to work.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatch prompts without PERSONA_CONTEXT trigger fallback file reads
Dispatch prompts without TOPIC_CONTEXT trigger fallback file reads
The fallback path produces identical behavior to the current implementation
